content_en:
ANAL SEX WITH WIVES Is anal sex with one’s wife is lawful?

Q: Is a husband permitted to have anal sex with his wife?

A: Having anal sex with one’s wife is strictly prohibited by the Holy Qur’an and the Prophetic Sunnah. This is the opinion of both the earlier and later scholars. It is termed as “Al-Lutiyyah Al-Sughra” [the lesser sodomy]. It is narrated after the Prophet (peace be upon him) that he said: “Allah does not shy of truth! Do not have anal sex with your wives!” In the Qur’an Allah says what means: “Your

wives are your tilth for you, so go to your tilth, when or how you will, and send (good deeds, or ask Allah to bestow upon you pious offspring) for your own selves beforehand.” (2:223)

According to Islam, man is permitted to have sex with his wife from any direction, so long as it is in her vagina. Should he have sex with her in the anus, and she accepts it, both of them should be given a discretionary punishment. If they then insist on this, they should be separated from one anther.

Nushuz {disobedience of the wife] A woman who fasts during the day and prays during the night and refuses her husband’s invitation to the bed

Q: A man has a wife how used to fast all day and observe prayer during the night and refuses her husband’s invitation to the bed. What is the judgment on this?

A: According to the consensus of Muslim scholars, this is permitted to her. Rather, she should answer the invitation of her husband to bed, as this is an obligation on her. Fasting all day and observing optional night prayer is something supererogatory. How then can it be given precedence over the obligation? In a hadith the Prophet

(peace be upon him) said: “A woman cannot observe Fasting in the presence of her husband except after taking his permission.”

If supererogatory Fasting of a woman in the presence of her husband in not permitted except after his permission, it is even worse if she refuses his invitation to bed. In the Two Sahihs it is narrated: “If a man invites his wife to bed but she refuses, the angels will curse her until the morning.” In the Qur’an Allah says what means: “Therefore the righteous women are devoutly obedient, and guard in (the husband’s) absence what Allah would have them guard.” (4:34) The right of the husband on his wife comes directly next to the rights of Allah and His Messenger (peace be upon him).

The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: “If ] were to order a human being to prostrate to a human being, | would have ordered the wife to prostrate to her husband. This is because he has a great right on her.” Once some women said to the Prophet (peace be upon him): Men fight in the cause of Allah, pay charity and do righteous deeds, which we cannot do. He replied: “Observing good matrimonial relationship with the husband equals all these

DIVORCE AND ANALOGOUS CASES

KHUL’ [DEMAND OF DIVORCE MADE BY THE WIFE IN RETURN FOR A

COMPENSATION GIVEN TO THE HUSBAND]

Khul’ in the Qur’an and Sunnah Q: What is Khul’ as in the light of the Qur’an and Sunnah?

A: The Khul’ mentioned in the Qur’an and Sunnah is the case when a wife hates her husband and wants to separate with him. She in turn repay to the husband the amount of dower or part of it, thus setting herself free from him as a captive is ransomed. If both parties still feel desire to each other, Khul’ in this case is innovative and alien to the precepts of Islam. But if the wife really hates her husband and chooses to separate with him, she may ransom herself by repaying the dower to him and absolving him from any liabilities due to her, on the condition that he divorces her by way of Khul’. This is according to the teachings of the Holy Qur’an and Sunnah, as well as the consensus of Muslim scholars.

Q: A woman who was averse to her husband, asked him to divorce her by way of Khul’, threatening that she might commit suicide, should he did not divorce her. Her guardian forced the husband to separate with her. She then

married to another husband. The first husband demanded her to return to him because the divorce he made was under coercion. However, she wanted to remain with the second husband. What is the judgment on this case?

A: If the first husband was really forced to separate with her, because of his negligence of his duties towards her, or because he inflicted harm on her without a rightful claim, in word or in deed, the separation will be valid. The second marriage will be valid also and she will be considered the wife of the second husband.

However, if coercion is made by beating the husband or confining him, despite his being good to her, separation will not be valid. Rather, if the wife feels averse to him, despite his being good to her, he may be demanded to separate with her, but not forced to do so. If he complies, it is alright. But if he refuses, the wife will be ordered to be patient, should there be no valid causes that annul

Accusing the wife of adultery

Q: A man accused his wife of adultery. He based his accusation on the incident that he sent her to attenda wedding, then he spied on her and found out that she was not there. She then denied this incident. When the husband told that to her family, and they asked her to confront him and defend herself, she refused, fearing that he might beat

her afterwards. She then went to the house of her maternal uncle. The husband thus made that a proof to cancel all her rights, claiming that she got out from his house without his permission. Is this a rightful claim to slip the wife of her rights?

A: Allah the Almighty says what means: "O you who believe! You are forbidden to inherit women against their will; and you should not treat them with harshness, that you may take away part of the Mahr you have given them, unless they commit open illegal sexual intercourse; and live with them honorably. If you dislike them, it may be that you dislike a thing and Allah brings through it a great deal of good." (4:19) It is not lawful for a man to prevent his wife from marrying another husband, in case life between them comes to a deadlock, in order to press her to remit him from the duties he owes her in the instance of divorce.

If the wife, however, commits indecency that is clearly proved, in such case the husband will have the right to prevent her and even beat her.

As for the family of the wife, they should ascertain which of the two parties has the right claim and support him/her in his/her case. If it is found out that the wife is the guilty party, she should ransom herself from him. If the husband claims that he sent her to a wedding, but she went elsewhere, he has the right to ask where she went. If he is told that she went to trustworthy people and those people

witnessed to that, or they admitted that she neither went to them nor went to the wedding, this is considered a valid reason to arose the doubt of the husband, and_ will be regarded as a support of the husband's claim.

Regarding the trousseau of the wife, she will be entitled to take it in all circumstances. If both the husband and wife reconcile, it will be better for them. Whenever the wife repents, it will be permissible for her husband to keep her under his custody. There will be no harm in this, as one who repents froma sin becomes like one who did not commit any sin. If they could not reach a settlement, the wife should absolve the husband of the amount of dower, thus the husband is to separate with her by virtue of Khul'. Allah the Almighty says what means: "Then if you fear that they would not be able to keep the limits ordained by Allah, then there is no sin on either of them if she gives back (the Mahr or a part of it) for her Khul'

The Khul' of a woman who has no guardian

Q: A previously married major woman who had no guardian except legal authorities, married through legal authorities, since she had no guardians. The wife then agreed with her husband to be divorced by way of Khul’, on the condition that she would absolve him of the amount

of dower without taking the permission of her guardian (i.e. legal authorities.) Are Khul' and absolution valid in this case?

A: If she is major enough to make a donation, her Khul' and absolution of dower will be valid, even without the

permission of legal authorities.

Q: A man said to his wife: "If you absolve me of your dower, you will be divorced," thus she absolved him, being not under a legal interdiction, and having no father or brother. She then claimed that she was insane, in order to render the absolution of the husband of the dower void. What is the judgment of this case?

A: Absolution of the dower cannot be rendered void by her mere claim of insanity. Rather, if proof is established that she was insane, but she was not under a legal interdiction, still the absolution of dower cannot be regarded effective, even if she manages her affairs by herself.

Divorce after the absolution of the dower Q: A woman absolved her husband of all her dower. He

then brought witnesses to testify that he divorced her due to 184

absolving him of the amount of dower. Is this divorce valid? Is it revocable or irrevocable?

A: If the husband agrees with the wife to divorce her in return for absolving him of the dower, such divorce will be effective and will be regarded as irrevocable. The same applies if he says to her: "Absolve me of the dower and I will divorce you," or "If you absolve me of the dower, I will divorce you," and suchlike phrases that indicate divorce conditioned by absolution of the dower. However, if she absolves him without the intention of asking for divorce, then he divorces her afterwards, divorce will be revocable.

A question is aroused whether the wife can revoke the absolution of the dower. Usually woman resorts to absolving her husband of the dower either out of her wish to part with him, or for fear of divorce, or that the husband might not marry another woman. In such instances, two narrations reported after Imam Ahmad, one confirming the opinion of revoking the absolution of the dower and one opposing it. If the wife willingly absolves the husband of the dower without any reason, not anticipating any return, in such case she will undoubtedly be entitled to revoke the
content_id:
HUBUNGAN SEKSUAL MELALUI DUBUR DENGAN ISTRI Apakah hubungan seksual melalui dubur dengan istri seseorang itu halal?

T: Apakah seorang suami diizinkan melakukan hubungan seksual melalui dubur dengan istrinya?

J: Melakukan hubungan seksual melalui dubur dengan istri seseorang dilarang secara tegas oleh Al-Qur'an yang mulia dan Sunnah Nabi. Ini adalah pendapat para ulama terdahulu maupun yang kemudian. Perbuatan ini disebut sebagai "Al-Lutiyyah Al-Sughra" [sodomi kecil]. Diriwayatkan dari Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bahwa beliau bersabda: "Allah tidak malu dari kebenaran! Janganlah kalian melakukan hubungan seksual melalui dubur istri-istri kalian!" Dalam Al-Qur'an Allah berfirman yang artinya: "Istri-istrimu

adalah ladang bagimu, maka datangilah ladangmu itu kapan saja dan dengan cara yang kamu kehendaki, dan dahulukanlah (amal-amal baik, atau mintalah kepada Allah agar menganugerahkan kepadamu keturunan yang saleh) untuk dirimu sendiri." (2:223)

Menurut Islam, seorang pria diizinkan berhubungan seksual dengan istrinya dari arah mana pun, selama itu melalui vaginanya. Apabila ia berhubungan seksual dengannya melalui dubur, dan sang istri menerimanya, keduanya harus diberikan hukuman ta'zir. Jika kemudian mereka bersikeras melakukan hal itu, keduanya harus dipisahkan satu sama lain.

Nushuz [ketidaktaatan istri] Seorang wanita yang berpuasa di siang hari dan shalat di malam hari namun menolak ajakan suaminya ke tempat tidur

T: Seorang pria memiliki istri yang terbiasa berpuasa sepanjang hari dan melakukan shalat di malam hari namun menolak ajakan suaminya ke tempat tidur. Apa hukumnya?

J: Menurut ijma' ulama Muslim, hal ini tidak diperbolehkan baginya. Bahkan, ia wajib memenuhi ajakan suaminya ke tempat tidur, karena itu adalah kewajiban atasnya. Berpuasa sepanjang hari dan melakukan shalat malam sunnah adalah sesuatu yang sifatnya sunnah. Bagaimana mungkin hal itu dapat didahulukan daripada kewajiban? Dalam sebuah hadith Nabi

(shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda: "Seorang wanita tidak boleh berpuasa di hadapan suaminya kecuali setelah mendapat izinnya."

Jika puasa sunnah seorang wanita di hadapan suaminya tidak diperbolehkan kecuali setelah mendapat izinnya, maka lebih buruk lagi jika ia menolak ajakan suaminya ke tempat tidur. Dalam dua kitab Shahih diriwayatkan: "Jika seorang pria mengajak istrinya ke tempat tidur namun ia menolak, para malaikat akan melaknatnya hingga pagi hari." Dalam Al-Qur'an Allah berfirman yang artinya: "Maka perempuan yang saleh adalah mereka yang taat (kepada Allah) dan menjaga diri ketika (suaminya) tidak ada, karena Allah telah menjaga (mereka)." (4:34) Hak suami atas istrinya berada tepat di bawah hak-hak Allah dan Rasul-Nya (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam).

Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda: "Seandainya aku memerintahkan seorang manusia untuk bersujud kepada manusia lain, niscaya aku perintahkan istri untuk bersujud kepada suaminya. Ini karena suami memiliki hak yang besar atasnya." Suatu ketika beberapa wanita berkata kepada Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam): Para pria berjihad di jalan Allah, membayar zakat, dan melakukan amal kebaikan yang tidak dapat kami lakukan. Beliau menjawab: "Menjaga hubungan pernikahan yang baik dengan suami setara dengan semua itu."

TALAK DAN HAL-HAL YANG SERUPA

KHUL' [TUNTUTAN CERAI YANG DIAJUKAN ISTRI SEBAGAI IMBALAN ATAS

KOMPENSASI YANG DIBERIKAN KEPADA SUAMI]

Khul' dalam Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah T: Apa itu Khul' dalam perspektif Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah?

J: Khul' yang disebutkan dalam Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah adalah kasus ketika seorang istri membenci suaminya dan ingin berpisah dengannya. Ia kemudian mengembalikan kepada suami sejumlah mahar atau sebagiannya, sehingga membebaskan dirinya darinya seperti seorang tawanan yang ditebus. Jika kedua pihak masih saling menginginkan satu sama lain, Khul' dalam hal ini adalah bid'ah dan asing dari ajaran Islam. Namun jika istri benar-benar membenci suaminya dan memilih untuk berpisah dengannya, ia dapat menebus dirinya dengan mengembalikan mahar kepadanya dan membebaskannya dari segala kewajiban yang menjadi haknya, dengan syarat bahwa ia menceraikannya melalui Khul'. Ini sesuai dengan ajaran Al-Qur'an yang mulia dan Sunnah, serta ijma' ulama Muslim.

T: Seorang wanita yang tidak menyukai suaminya, memintanya untuk menceraikannya melalui Khul', mengancam bahwa ia mungkin akan bunuh diri jika suaminya tidak menceraikannya. Walinya memaksa suami untuk berpisah dengannya. Ia kemudian

menikah dengan suami lain. Suami pertama menuntut agar ia kembali kepadanya karena talak yang ia ucapkan dilakukan di bawah paksaan. Namun, ia ingin tetap bersama suami kedua. Apa hukumnya dalam kasus ini?

J: Jika suami pertama benar-benar dipaksa untuk berpisah dengannya, karena kelalaiannya dalam menunaikan kewajibannya terhadapnya, atau karena ia menyakitinya tanpa alasan yang benar, baik dalam perkataan maupun perbuatan, maka pemisahan tersebut sah. Pernikahan kedua juga akan sah dan ia dianggap sebagai istri dari suami kedua.

Namun, jika paksaan dilakukan dengan memukul suami atau mengurungnya, meskipun ia telah berbuat baik kepadanya, maka pemisahan tersebut tidak sah. Sebaliknya, jika istri merasa tidak suka kepadanya, meskipun ia telah berbuat baik kepadanya, ia boleh diminta untuk berpisah dengannya, tetapi tidak boleh dipaksa untuk melakukannya. Jika ia menuruti permintaan itu, maka itu baik. Namun jika ia menolak, maka istri akan diperintahkan untuk bersabar, selama tidak ada alasan-alasan sah yang membatalkan

Menuduh istri berzina

T: Seorang pria menuduh istrinya berzina. Ia mendasarkan tuduhannya pada kejadian bahwa ia mengutusnya untuk menghadiri pernikahan, kemudian ia memata-matainya dan mendapati bahwa ia tidak berada di sana. Istri kemudian menyangkal kejadian ini. Ketika suami menceritakan hal itu kepada keluarganya, dan mereka memintanya untuk mengkonfrontasinya dan membela diri, ia menolak, karena takut suaminya akan memukulnya

setelah itu. Ia kemudian pergi ke rumah paman dari pihak ibunya. Suami kemudian menjadikan hal itu sebagai bukti untuk membatalkan semua haknya, dengan mengklaim bahwa ia keluar dari rumahnya tanpa izinnya. Apakah ini klaim yang sah untuk merampas hak-hak istri?

J: Allah Yang Maha Kuasa berfirman yang artinya: "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Tidak halal bagi kamu mewarisi perempuan dengan jalan paksa dan janganlah kamu menyusahkan mereka karena hendak mengambil kembali sebagian dari apa yang telah kamu berikan kepadanya, kecuali apabila mereka melakukan perbuatan keji yang nyata. Dan bergaullah dengan mereka secara patut. Jika kamu tidak menyukai mereka, (maka bersabarlah) karena boleh jadi kamu tidak menyukai sesuatu, padahal Allah menjadikan kebaikan yang banyak padanya." (4:19) Tidak halal bagi seorang pria untuk menghalangi istrinya untuk menikah dengan pria lain, jika kehidupan di antara mereka sudah buntu, dengan maksud untuk menekannya agar membebaskannya dari kewajiban-kewajiban yang ia miliki terhadapnya dalam kasus talak.

Namun, jika istri melakukan perbuatan keji yang terbukti jelas, dalam kasus tersebut suami berhak untuk menghalanginya bahkan memukulnya.

Adapun keluarga istri, mereka harus memastikan pihak mana yang memiliki klaim yang benar dan mendukungnya dalam kasusnya. Jika ternyata istri adalah pihak yang bersalah, ia harus menebus dirinya sendiri darinya. Jika suami mengklaim bahwa ia mengutusnya ke sebuah pernikahan, tetapi ia pergi ke tempat lain, ia berhak untuk menanyakan ke mana ia pergi. Jika diberitahu bahwa ia pergi ke orang-orang yang terpercaya dan orang-orang tersebut

bersaksi untuk itu, atau mereka mengakui bahwa ia tidak pergi kepada mereka maupun tidak pergi ke pernikahan tersebut, ini dianggap sebagai alasan yang sah untuk menimbulkan keraguan pada suami, dan akan dianggap sebagai dukungan atas klaim suami.

Mengenai harta bawaan istri, ia berhak untuk mengambilnya dalam segala keadaan. Jika suami dan istri berdamai, itu akan lebih baik bagi mereka. Kapan pun istri bertaubat, maka diperbolehkan bagi suaminya untuk tetap mempertahankannya dalam perwaliannya. Tidak ada salahnya dalam hal ini, karena orang yang bertaubat dari suatu dosa adalah seperti orang yang tidak pernah melakukan dosa tersebut. Jika mereka tidak dapat mencapai penyelesaian, istri harus membebaskan suami dari jumlah mahar, sehingga suami harus berpisah dengannya melalui Khul'. Allah Yang Maha Kuasa berfirman yang artinya: "Kemudian jika kamu khawatir bahwa keduanya tidak mampu menjalankan hukum-hukum Allah, maka tidak ada dosa atas keduanya tentang bayaran yang diberikan oleh istri untuk menebus dirinya (Khul')."

Khul' seorang wanita yang tidak memiliki wali

T: Seorang wanita yang telah menikah sebelumnya dan sudah dewasa yang tidak memiliki wali selain otoritas hukum, menikah melalui otoritas hukum, karena ia tidak memiliki wali. Istri kemudian bersepakat dengan suaminya untuk diceraikan melalui Khul', dengan syarat bahwa ia akan membebaskan suami dari jumlah

mahar tanpa mengambil izin dari walinya (yaitu otoritas hukum). Apakah Khul' dan pembebasan tersebut sah dalam kasus ini?

J: Jika ia cukup dewasa untuk melakukan donasi, maka khul' dan pembebasan maharnya akan sah, meskipun tanpa

izin dari pihak berwenang.

T: Seorang suami berkata kepada istrinya: "Jika engkau membebaskan aku dari maharmu, maka engkau dicerai," lalu ia membebaskannya, sedangkan ia tidak berada di bawah pengampuan hukum, dan tidak memiliki ayah maupun saudara laki-laki. Kemudian ia mengklaim bahwa dirinya gila, dengan tujuan membatalkan pembebasan suami dari mahar. Apa hukum kasus ini?

J: Pembebasan mahar tidak dapat dibatalkan hanya dengan klaimnya bahwa ia gila. Bahkan jika terbukti bahwa ia memang gila, namun tidak berada di bawah pengampuan hukum, maka pembebasan mahar tetap tidak dapat dianggap sah, meskipun ia mengurus urusannya sendiri.

Talak setelah pembebasan mahar T: Seorang istri membebaskan suaminya dari seluruh maharnya. Kemudian ia

mendatangkan saksi-saksi untuk bersaksi bahwa ia menceraikannya karena pembebasan dari jumlah mahar. Apakah talak ini sah? Apakah talak ini raj'i ataukah ba'in?

J: Jika suami bersepakat dengan istri untuk menceraikannya sebagai imbalan atas pembebasan dari mahar, maka talak tersebut berlaku dan dianggap sebagai talak ba'in. Hal yang sama berlaku jika ia berkata kepadanya: "Bebaskan aku dari mahar dan aku akan menceraikanmu," atau "Jika engkau membebaskan aku dari mahar, maka aku akan menceraikanmu," dan kalimat-kalimat semisalnya yang menunjukkan talak yang disyaratkan dengan pembebasan mahar. Namun, jika ia membebaskannya tanpa niat meminta talak, kemudian ia menceraikannya setelah itu, maka talak tersebut adalah talak raj'i.

Timbul pertanyaan apakah istri dapat mencabut kembali pembebasan mahar. Biasanya seorang wanita mengambil jalan pembebasan suami dari mahar, baik karena keinginannya untuk berpisah darinya, atau karena takut ditalak, atau agar suami tidak menikah dengan wanita lain. Dalam kondisi-kondisi seperti ini, terdapat dua riwayat dari Imam Ahmad, satu mengonfirmasi pendapat bolehnya mencabut pembebasan mahar dan satu lagi menentangnya. Jika istri dengan sukarela membebaskan suami dari mahar tanpa alasan apapun, tidak mengharapkan imbalan apapun, maka dalam hal ini ia tidak diragukan lagi berhak untuk mencabut kembali
content_ar: ANAL SEX WITH WIVES Is anal sex with one’s الزوجة is lawful? Q: Is a الزوج permitted to have anal sex with his الزوجة? A: Having anal sex with one’s الزوجة is strictly prohibited by the Holy Qur’an and the Prophetic السنة. This is the opinion of both the earlier and later العلماء. It is termed as “Al-Lutiyyah Al-Sughra” [the lesser sodomy]. روي بعد النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) that قال: “الله does not shy of الحق! Do not have anal sex with your wives!” In the Qur’an الله says what means: “Your wives are your tilth for you, لذا go to your tilth, عندما or how you will, and send (الخير deeds, or ask الله to bestow upon you pious offspring) for your own selves beforehand.” (2:223) وفقاً لـ الإسلام, man is permitted to have sex with his الزوجة from any direction, لذا long as it is in her vagina. Should he have sex with her in the anus, and she accepts it, both of them should be given a discretionary العقاب. إذا they then insist on this, they should be separated from one anther. Nushuz {disobedience of the الزوجة] A woman who fasts خلال the day and prays خلال the night and refuses her الزوج’s invitation to the bed Q: A man has a الزوجة how used to fast all day and observe الصلاة خلال the night and refuses her الزوج’s invitation to the bed. What is the judgment on this? A: وفقاً لـ the consensus of مسلم العلماء, this is permitted to her. Rather, she should answer the invitation of her الزوج to bed, as this is an الالتزام on her. الصيام all day and observing optional night الصلاة is something supererogatory. How then can it be given precedence over the الالتزام? In a الحديث النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) said: “A woman cannot observe الصيام in the presence of her الزوج except بعد taking his permission.” إذا supererogatory الصيام of a woman in the presence of her الزوج in not permitted except بعد his permission, it is even worse إذا she refuses his invitation to bed. In the Two Sahihs روي: “إذا a man invites his الزوجة to bed but she refuses, the الملائكة will curse her حتى the morning.” In the Qur’an الله says what means: “لذلك the الصالح women are devoutly obedient, and guard in (the الزوج’s) absence what الله would have them guard.” (4:34) The الحق of the الزوج on his الزوجة comes directly next to the الحقوق of الله and His Messenger (ﷺ). النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) said: “إذا ] were to order a human being to prostrate to a human being, | would have ordered the الزوجة to prostrate to her الزوج. This is لأن he has a great الحق on her.” حين some women said to النبي ﷺ (ﷺ): Men fight in the cause of الله, pay الصدقة and do الصالح deeds, which we cannot do. أجاب: “Observing الخير matrimonial relationship with the الزوج equals all these DIVORCE AND ANALOGOUS CASES KHUL’ [DEMAND OF DIVORCE MADE BY THE الزوجة IN RETURN FOR A COMPENSATION GIVEN TO THE الزوج] Khul’ in the Qur’an and السنة Q: What is Khul’ as in the light of the Qur’an and السنة? A: The Khul’ mentioned in the Qur’an and السنة is the case عندما a الزوجة hates her الزوج and wants to separate with him. She in turn repay to the الزوج the amount of dower or part of it, وهكذا setting herself free from him as a captive is ransomed. إذا both parties still feel desire to بعضهم بعضاً, Khul’ in this case is innovative and alien to the precepts of الإسلام. But إذا the الزوجة really hates her الزوج and chooses to separate with him, she may ransom herself by repaying the dower to him and absolving him from any liabilities بسبب her, on the condition that he divorces her by الطريق of Khul’. This is وفقاً لـ the teachings of the Holy Qur’an and السنة, as well as the consensus of مسلم العلماء. Q: A woman who was averse to her الزوج, asked him to divorce her by الطريق of Khul’, threatening that she might commit suicide, should he did not divorce her. Her guardian forced the الزوج to separate with her. She then married to another الزوج. The أولاً الزوج demanded her to return to him لأن the divorce he made was under coercion. غير أن, she wanted to remain with the ثانياً الزوج. What is the judgment on this case? A: إذا the أولاً الزوج was really forced to separate with her, لأن of his negligence of his duties towards her, or لأن he inflicted harm on her without a rightful claim, in word or in deed, the separation will be valid. The ثانياً marriage will be valid أيضاً and she will be considered the الزوجة of the ثانياً الزوج. غير أن, إذا coercion is made by beating the الزوج or confining him, رغم his being الخير to her, separation will not be valid. Rather, إذا the الزوجة feels averse to him, رغم his being الخير to her, he may be demanded to separate with her, but not forced to do لذا. إذا he complies, it is alright. But إذا he refuses, the الزوجة will be ordered to be patient, should there be no valid causes that annul Accusing the الزوجة of adultery Q: A man accused his الزوجة of adultery. He based his accusation on the incident that he sent her to attenda wedding, then he spied on her and found out that she was not there. She then denied this incident. عندما the الزوج told that to her الأسرة, and they asked her to confront him and defend herself, she refused, fearing that he might beat her afterwards. She then went to the house of her maternal uncle. The الزوج وهكذا made that a proof to cancel all her الحقوق, claiming that she got out from his house without his permission. Is this a rightful claim to slip the الزوجة of her الحقوق? A: الله the Almighty says what means: "O you who believe! You are forbidden to inherit women against their will; and you should not treat them with harshness, that you may take away part of the Mahr you have given them, إلا إذا they commit open illegal sexual intercourse; and live with them honorably. إذا you dislike them, it may be that you dislike a thing and الله brings through it a great deal of الخير." (4:19) It is not lawful for a man to prevent his الزوجة from marrying another الزوج, in case الحياة between them comes to a deadlock, من أجل press her to remit him from the duties he owes her in the instance of divorce. إذا the الزوجة, غير أن, commits indecency that is clearly proved, in such case the الزوج will have the الحق to prevent her and even beat her. أما the الأسرة of the الزوجة, they should ascertain which of the two parties has the الحق claim and support him/her in his/her case. إذا it is found out that the الزوجة is the guilty party, she should ransom herself from him. إذا the الزوج claims that he sent her to a wedding, but she went elsewhere, he has the الحق to ask where she went. إذا he is told that she went to trustworthy people and those people witnessed to that, or they admitted that she neither went to them nor went to the wedding, this is considered a valid العقل to arose the doubt of the الزوج, and_ will be regarded as a support of the الزوج's claim. فيما يخص the trousseau of the الزوجة, she will be entitled to take it in all circumstances. إذا both the الزوج and الزوجة reconcile, it will be better for them. كلما the الزوجة repents, it will be permissible for her الزوج to keep her under his custody. There will be no harm in this, as one who repents froma الذنب becomes like one who did not commit any الذنب. إذا they could not reach a settlement, the الزوجة should absolve the الزوج of the amount of dower, وهكذا the الزوج is to separate with her by الفضيلة of Khul'. الله the Almighty says what means: "Then إذا you الخوف that they would not be able to keep the limits ordained by الله, then there is no الذنب on either of them إذا she gives back (the Mahr or a part of it) for her Khul' The Khul' of a woman who has no guardian Q: A previously married major woman who had no guardian except legal authorities, married through legal authorities, بما أن she had no guardians. The الزوجة then agreed with her الزوج to be divorced by الطريق of Khul’, on the condition that she would absolve him of the amount of dower without taking the permission of her guardian (i.e. legal authorities.) Are Khul' and absolution valid in this case? A: إذا she is major enough to make a donation, her Khul' and absolution of dower will be valid, even without the permission of legal authorities. Q: A man said to his الزوجة: "إذا you absolve me of your dower, you will be divorced," وهكذا she absolved him, being not under a legal interdiction, and having no الأب or الأخ. She then claimed that she was insane, من أجل render the absolution of the الزوج of the dower void. What is the judgment of this case? A: Absolution of the dower cannot be rendered void by her mere claim of insanity. Rather, إذا proof is established that she was insane, but she was not under a legal interdiction, still the absolution of dower cannot be regarded effective, even إذا she manages her affairs by herself. Divorce بعد the absolution of the dower Q: A woman absolved her الزوج of all her dower. He then brought witnesses to testify that he divorced her بسبب 184 absolving him of the amount of dower. Is this divorce valid? Is it revocable or irrevocable? A: إذا the الزوج agrees with the الزوجة to divorce her in return for absolving him of the dower, such divorce will be effective and will be regarded as irrevocable. The same applies إذا he says to her: "Absolve me of the dower and I will divorce you," or "إذا you absolve me of the dower, I will divorce you," and suchlike phrases that indicate divorce conditioned by absolution of the dower. غير أن, إذا she absolves him without the intention of asking for divorce, then he divorces her afterwards, divorce will be revocable. A question is aroused whether the الزوجة can revoke the absolution of the dower. Usually woman resorts to absolving her الزوج of the dower either out of her wish to part with him, or for الخوف of divorce, or that the الزوج might not marry another woman. In such instances, two narrations reported بعد الإمام أحمد, one confirming the opinion of revoking the absolution of the dower and one opposing it. إذا the الزوجة willingly absolves the الزوج of the dower without any العقل, not anticipating any return, in such case she will undoubtedly be entitled to revoke the
